codeReview.summary: The api-routes feature is fully implemented and meets all requirements. Both /api/calls and /api/calls/stats route handlers are correctly implemented with proper TypeScript types, error handling, and comprehensive test coverage. The implementation follows Next.js App Router conventions and properly mocks the Google Sheets service in tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill procedure expects TDD with tests written first, but the worker session df1e2224-5703-4b18-a437-f443f574bc7b noted that the feature was already implemented in an earlier commit. The skill should have a verification-only branch for cases where implementation already exists.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff from worker session df1e2224-5703-4b18-a437-f443f574bc7b shows skillFeedback.followedProcedure: false with deviation: 'Did not create new tests because the feature was already fully implemented with route tests in commit 80bd170'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The API routes require valid Google Sheets credentials to return actual data. Manual testing via curl returned 500 errors with 'Failed to fetch calls' due to missing/invalid credentials, which is expected behavior but means the happy path can't be verified without proper environment setup.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker transcript shows: curl.exe -s http://localhost:3000/api/calls returned '{"error":"Failed to fetch calls"}' with status 500, confirming error response shape works correctly.

summary: The api-routes feature has been successfully implemented with two Next.js API route handlers: (1) GET /api/calls returns all calls sorted reverse-chronologically with proper error handling, (2) GET /api/calls/stats returns aggregated statistics including totalCalls, answeredCount, voicemailCount, qualifiedLeads, averageDuration, and callsPerDay for the last 14 days. Both routes handle empty data correctly (returning [] or zeroed stats) and return structured errors with 500 status on API failures. The tests are comprehensive, covering happy path, empty data, and error cases with proper mocking of the Google Sheets service. All 11 tests pass, TypeScript type-checking passes, and linting passes.
